name: @chbrain/khai-engine-document
description: khai engine content, the document domain: an inscription that fixes a claim into durable form and travels unchanged -- what it fixes, who bears it, and how it is inscribed and carried.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Document as piece: an inscription that fixes a claim into durable form and travels unchanged into contexts its author never occupies -- the record, contract, certificate, or file whose authority is that it says the same thing everywhere it goes.

card.wire: Attaches at Piece. The anchor (piece_document.md) declares the document as what it is in the room: an inscription fixed once and carried unchanged, so its claim reads the same wherever it is opened. The law makes fixity itself a thing the scene can carry.
card.issue: The document, read beneath the inscription. One position names who holds and wields its authority (the bearer); two processes name what happens around it -- the act of fixing a claim into durable form (inscription) and its unchanged movement into contexts its author never occupies (circulation). The inscription is the piece; the members are the authority it carries.
card.require: Two altitudes hold the engine. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor, so a document is read as fixed and portable wherever it sits. The link: the persona who holds it links the bearer stance and the process it is in under Projection.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and a real bearer position or document process is linked. Whether the document truly carries the fixed authority the scene needs is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then place the document: name the claim it fixes, who bears it, and which process it is in -- being inscribed, or already circulating unchanged. Read it in the fact that no one present wrote it, yet everyone treats it as settled.
